paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement (SS) sets out the Prudential Regulation Authority's (PRA) expectations in respect of how insurers should calculate their technical provisions, expanding on the rules set out
This SS is relevant to all UK Solvency II firms, the Society of Lloyd’s, its members and managing agents. This SS refers to these collectively as ‘insurers’ or ‘firms’ unless otherwise specified.

In addition, part 2 of this SS is also relevant to third-country insurance undertakings that have a UK branch (third-country branch undertakings) who should read any reference to ‘technical provisions

This SS seeks to advance the PRA’s statutory objective of promoting the safety and soundness of the firms it regulates by setting out the PRA’s expectations of how insurers should calculate their tech
In line with Technical Provisions – Further Requirements 27 (Proportionality), insurers are expected to ensure
Furthermore, Technical Provisions 2 (Calculation of Technical Provisions) sets out general principles firms should comply with when

It is important that firms consider carefully whether the methods used in their technical provisions calculations comply with the Technical Provisions rules,2 including that they are in fact proportio

Where appropriate, firms may calculate the amounts recoverable from reinsurance contracts and special purpose vehicles before adjusting those amounts to take account of the expected loss due to defaul

Firms may use methods to derive the unadjusted net best estimate from the gross best estimate without an explicit projection of the cash flows underlying the amounts recoverable from reinsurance contr

Where appropriate, firms may calculate the best estimate of long-term insurance business obligations with an arrangement by which the firm has the right or the obligation to adjust the future premiums

Where appropriate, firms may calculate the adjustment for expected losses due to default of the counterparty, referred to in [Technical Provisions 11.1](/pra-rules/technical-provisions#865de775dd83496
Where appropriate, firms may use simplified methods when they calculate the risk margin, including one or more of the following:

Where appropriate, firms may derive the risk margin for calculations that need to be performed quarterly from the result of an earlier calculation of the risk margin without an explicit calculation of

section_headings.text: SS8/24 – Solvency II: Calculation of technical provisions
Simplified calculation of recoverables from reinsurance contracts and special purpose vehicles
Simplified calculation of the best estimate for insurance obligations with premium adjustment mechanism
Simplified calculation of the calculation of the counterparty default adjustment
Simplified calculation of the risk margin
Calculations of the risk margin during the financial year
